entries.receipt: 53.7% there vs 50.1% overall, 297 games of evidence

leanInto.name: Khaimera
leanInto.engineCoachLine: Sim-optimal for skirmish uptime: first spike lands around minute 12 (Viper), third item by minute 25. Take Demiurge: +33.6% on 20-second fights at minute 15.

plan: Keep queueing jungle as primary — it is already your usual queue AND your best role at 53.7% over 297 games (your overall is 50.1%).
Stop queueing carry: 41.8% over 184 games is costing real VP. When auto-filled there, play your safest comfort pick, not a learning pick.
Comfort-hero rule for the climb: Khaimera. Compared with the average player on the same hero, you win about 3.0 more games per 100 on it.
Your champion pool is 10+ heroes wide and your top three are only 41% of your games. Gold-to-Platinum climbs are almost always pool-narrowing stories: aim for 70%+ of games on your top three.
The math: you are at 326 VP; Platinum III starts at 900. Your all-time peak is 692.
honesty: personal winrates are shrunk toward your own average (small heater samples do not count as mains)
field baselines are all-ranks aggregates from our current-patch match sample, not Gold-bracket-specific yet
winrate edges are observational; they say where you win, not why

insights.title: Greystone is not just your best hero, it is your most disciplined
insights.finding: On Greystone you die 3.4/game vs your 4.9 average. The winrate edge is partly a discipline edge; that habit can travel to other picks.
insights.receipt: 93 games on Greystone, 50.8% shrunk vs field 47.6%

insights.title: Vision is your cheapest upgrade
insights.finding: 4.7 wards per game, 56% of the squad average. Wards cost nothing and you are leaving them in the shop.
insights.receipt: 4,002 placed + 243 cleared over 899 games; squad average 8.4/game

insights.title: Khaimera is the long-term relationship
insights.finding: 163 games and you genuinely out-pilot the field on Khaimera — about 3.0 more wins per 100 games than the average Khaimera player. The hours show.

insights.title: Mr_Meat31 brings out your best
insights.finding: Your record alongside Mr_Meat31 is 54% over 237 ranked games — your strongest pairing in the stack. When only two of you queue, make it this one.

insights.title: For the group chat
insights.finding: A longest killing spree of 16 in real 5v5s (plus 2 more in ARAM and brawls — the group chat can rule on whether those count). Xeebs has receipts.

coachReasoning.assessment: Khaimera is carrying you — both recent wins came on him (8/6/5 and 7/5/12) — but your losses are death spikes, 11 on Yin jungle (6/11/6) and 7 on Khaimera, well above your norms. The single biggest lever is fighting only when your passive is healed up and buying the wards you keep skipping, since vision is your cheapest available upgrade.
plan: On Khaimera, only commit to a fight when your passive lifesteal and health are topped — in the 7-death losing game you engaged low; use your leap to start ganks from full and your ultimate to lock the target you can actually kill, not to chase.

Buy vision every back on Khaimera — you average just 4.7 wards a game (56% of the squad); warding your gank paths and objectives turns your strong ganks into safe ones and cuts the deaths that sank recent games.

On Khaimera build to your minute-12 spike (Viper) and third item by 25, and take Demiurge for a big boost to your 20-second skirmishes at 15 — then hunt short, repeated fights rather than one long over-extend.
On Greystone, lean into the discipline that already shows — you die just 3.4 per game on him versus your 4.9 average; carry that patience back to Khaimera and Yin so your death spikes shrink.
On Yin, the 6/11/6 game was 11 deaths — use your dash to disengage after a combo instead of diving again; play for clean picks, then reset, rather than staying in the fight.

insights.title: Khaimera is the whole climb
insights.finding: 65.6% over 163 games and about 3.0 more wins per 100 than the average Khaimera; both recent wins were his. Spamming him is worth roughly 3.8 more wins per 100 games.

insights.title: Wards are free rating
insights.finding: You place 4.7 wards a game, just 56% of the squad average — the wards are sitting in the shop; buying them prevents the ganks-gone-wrong that cost you.

insights.title: Fight healthy, not often-low
insights.finding: Your losses were death spikes — 11 on Yin, 7 on Khaimera versus your norms; Khaimera thrives on repeated short fights when topped up, so re-engage only after your passive heals you.

insights.title: Greystone proves the habit
insights.finding: On Greystone you die 3.4 per game versus your 4.9 average — the discipline is already in your hands; transfer that spacing to Khaimera and the winrate follows.

coachReasoningSource: in-session pred-scout-coach agent, grounded in the report + the player’s recent reviewed games; every number ground-checked, failing lines dropped
